entries.title: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS MANUAL ON REVELATION on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: QUESTION: And he took me up in spirit to a great mountain: and he shewed me the holy city Jerusalem. ANSWER: After the fall of Babylon, the holy city, which is the bride of the Lamb, is seen on the mountain, for the stone cut out of a mountain without hands has broken in pieces the effigy of worldly glory, and has grown into a great mountain, and filled the whole world. [Dan. 2:34-35] THERE FOLLOWS: Coming down out of heaven from God, having the glory of God. ANSWER: It will indeed appear more beautiful when, by the Spirit by which its bridegroom is believed to have been conceived and engendered, it has gained fully to have a heavenly appearance.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE APOCALYPSE 21:11 on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: The radiance of the church is Christ, who here is depicted as a jasper and clear as crystal to indicate that he is unfading and life-giving and pure. He is also depicted by means of other images. For the manifold diversity of his generosity for us cannot be described merely through the illustration of one form.

entries.title: TRACTATE ON THE APOCALYPSE 21:10-11 on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: We read of this very city in the prophets: "The sun shall be no more your light by day, nor shall the brightness of the moon give you light. But the Lord will be everlasting light, and your God will be your glory." … As the brilliance of a stone shines neither in itself nor from the outside, but it is translucent by the clarity of its nature, so this city is described as illumined by no radiance of the stars but as invisibly illumined by the light of God alone. The shining clarity of the crystal signifies that in the city the grace of baptism shines with a reddish hue.

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: Like a jasper stone, clear as crystal. Jasper, for the brightness of virtues; crystal, for the inner purity of the mind and unfeigned faith (1 Tim. 1).

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: Her light was like a precious stone. The precious stone is Christ, who says: The glory which you have given me I have given to them (John 17).

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: Her radiance, that is, the Sun of righteousness Christ (Mal. 4:2), is said to be like a jasper stone. The jasper, as was said above, being somewhat green, signifies the life-giving and life-sustaining Christ, "who opens his hand and fills every living thing with good will" (Ps. 145:16); for green is considered the source of all earthly food. But the jasper was also clear as crystal, showing the purity and holiness of Christ; "for he committed no sin, and deceit was not found in his mouth," (Isa. 53:9) according to the prophecies of Isaiah. And Christ again is the wall of the saints, that is, of his church, being our bulwark and defense and help.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE APOCALYPSE 21:11 on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: [To which bride] the Lord says, "You are the light of the world." This expression pertains to the entire body of Christ, although the apostles heard it from the mouth of the Truth, since they were first in both time and merit.… Although the jasper and the crystal are different kinds of stones with different colors, nonetheless here he aptly conjoins both of them for the sake of the metaphor. He compares them to a most precious stone, so that it would be understood to refer to Christ, of whom the blessed Peter spoke: "a cornerstone precious, laid firm in the foundation."

entries.title: Shepherd of Hermas, Similitude 9 on revelation 21:11
entries.excerpt: And the gate glittered to such a degree under the sunbeams, that I marvelled at the splendour of the gate; and round about the gate stood twelve virgins.
